findings.evidence: `evenPrimeDiscriminantRadicand` is just `D / 4` at line 63; Mathlib already has the general integer division cancellation API (`Int.ediv_mul_cancel`, grep hit `mathlib/Mathlib/Data/Int/GCD.lean:205`).
findings.fix: Add/prove the general statement first, e.g. `theorem eq_four_mul_evenPrimeDiscriminantRadicand_of_four_dvd {D : ℤ} (hD : (4 : ℤ) ∣ D) : D = 4 * evenPrimeDiscriminantRadicand D`, using `Int.ediv_mul_cancel`, then make the current lemma a corollary by proving `4 ∣ D` from the three cases.
findings.issue: `evenPrimeDiscriminant_eq_four_mul_radicand` assumes `IsEvenPrimeDiscriminant D`, but the conclusion is the standard division reconstruction for any integer divisible by `4`.

summary: One reconstruction lemma is stated at the special even-prime-discriminant level even though the arithmetic fact only needs divisibility by 4; expose the general lemma and derive the finite-case wrapper from it.
